response.justification: The candidate is an experienced full-stack engineer with a strong web and product background, but he does not meet the core requirements of this role. There is no indication of native-level Japanese proficiency (a hard requirement), no Linux kernel investigation or modification experience, and no background in L2/L3 networking, switch products, or security technologies like MACsec or 802.1X. His C experience is limited to a brief stint at National Instruments and a thesis project, far from the strong UNIX C proficiency required for embedded firmware development.
strengths: 19+ years of broad software development experience across web, mobile, desktop, and some embedded contexts
Some C/C++ exposure (NI LabWindows/CVI components) and Linux familiarity
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, testing culture, open-source contributions
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency listed — a mandatory requirement for the role
No experience with Linux kernel modification, embedded networking firmware, or LAN-switch products
Career centered on web/full-stack (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang), not security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, encryption) or chip-vendor SDKs

key_factors.factor: No embedded networking / kernel / security domain experience
Long general software engineering track record
